title: Scholarship essays — Nov 2020 senior-year hustle map
html:
TL;DR

  • Compact senior-year action map listing 4 scholarship apps (osu, Renaissance, Ford, jcef) alongside Amazon income hustle, marketing-agency-side hustle ("local doordash, SEO enhancement"), pre-college "completing adventures before farewell" plan (college admissions context) (18th bday trip, foreign country visit, walk a marathon), and the recurring "establish self-checks/Meditation" line.
  • The Renaissance + Ford pair show up again in 2021 01 23 the al forthans and 2023 02 28 ford family foundation emilio draft — these scholarships are formative.
  • "Dad on DoorDash / Grubhub and Postmates??" — earliest documented attempt to leverage his dad into the gig economy. Same pattern as helping mom with FBA via "quarantine business for dad" line.
  • Walking-a-marathon was already on the bucket list in 2020 — track if he ever did it.

Key quotes

"-Completing Adventures before farewell / 18 bday ever trip / Foreign country visit / Walking a marathon"

"-Source of Income, marketing / local doordash / SEO enhancement"

"-Dad on DoorDash / Grubhub and Postmates??"

Why it matters in the graph

  • The "SEO enhancement" income-hustle line in 2020 is the earliest crystallization of the ai search optimization/SEO instinct that would mature into rankett in 2026.
  • Confirms multi-scholarship strategy: he applied to OSU, Renaissance, Ford, and JCEF in parallel. The Ford scholarship hit (2025 10 20 quality resume confirms full-ride).
  • "Establish self-checks / Meditation / Observe situation and outcomes" — an early appearance of the disconnected from outcome mental discipline he'd later articulate.

Open questions

  • Did the dad-on-DoorDash plan ever materialize?
  • Did he walk the marathon? Foreign country visit before college? (The Mexico/family trips were typically family-organized, not solo.)

Wikilinks

osu · ford family foundation · jcef · ai search optimization · bridge income strategies · disconnected from outcome
